list.name: Changes
list.comment: 13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number

task.summary: Sixth commit 05/16/2026: Reordered directory, put non-final testing data into /process_time_testing_data

Eighth commit 05/25/2026: 1. Sorted data, removed duplicates 2. Fixed algorithmic problems of failing primes caused by ignoring supersingular elliptic curves when searching for triples 3. Performed hypothesis testing on number of trials for primes with the same digit length, conclusion: REJECT H0 that all numbers are generated from the same parameter. This indicates that the difficulty of finding triple for primes similar in size differs significantly and each prime might have unique properties to discover.

Ninth commit 06/01/2026: 1. Refined the trials counting algorithm to only include trials finding A 2. Refined the trials recording algorithm for better accuracy (the previous lc*threads tends to overestimate trials count) 3. Included two parameters (a) (b) in difficulty_parameters.py for evaluating difficulty 4. Initial test for Order-Restricted LRT on primes within a small range (regional largest=1.1*smallest) for H0: p1>=p2>=...>=pk vs H1: unconstrained, rejection rate ranges from 10%-30%
Tenth Commit 06/01/2026: Fixed problems for failure in uploading lrt_data
Eleventh Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
12th Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number

MESSAGE.value: Sixth commit 05/16/2026: Reordered directory, put non-final testing data into /process_time_testing_data

Eighth commit 05/25/2026: 1. Sorted data, removed duplicates 2. Fixed algorithmic problems of failing primes caused by ignoring supersingular elliptic curves when searching for triples 3. Performed hypothesis testing on number of trials for primes with the same digit length, conclusion: REJECT H0 that all numbers are generated from the same parameter. This indicates that the difficulty of finding triple for primes similar in size differs significantly and each prime might have unique properties to discover.

Ninth commit 06/01/2026: 1. Refined the trials counting algorithm to only include trials finding A 2. Refined the trials recording algorithm for better accuracy (the previous lc*threads tends to overestimate trials count) 3. Included two parameters (a) (b) in difficulty_parameters.py for evaluating difficulty 4. Initial test for Order-Restricted LRT on primes within a small range (regional largest=1.1*smallest) for H0: p1>=p2>=...>=pk vs H1: unconstrained, rejection rate ranges from 10%-30%
Tenth Commit 06/01/2026: Fixed problems for failure in uploading lrt_data
Eleventh Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
12th Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: 13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number
